steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The blank must insert a non-restrictive appositive — ‘in this case, Herman Chernoff’ — between ‘polymath’ and the relative clause ‘who was not the first to identify the mathematical bound.’ Non-restrictive appositives must be set off with matching punctuation on both sides.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — polymath—in this case, Herman Chernoff—: Uses symmetrical em-dashes on both sides of the appositive. Correct. ✓
  • Choice B — polymath: in this case, Herman Chernoff,: Opens with a colon and closes with a comma — mismatched punctuation. ✗
  • Choice C — polymath—in this case, Herman Chernoff,: Opens with an em-dash but closes with a comma — mismatched punctuation. ✗
  • Choice D — polymath in this case, Herman Chernoff,: No opening punctuation at all, so the appositive is not properly set off. ✗
That is the correct answer.
